commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ea1e5c82c49ddbbe3461841242e90adb207cd013)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new read-tool encoding helper with BOM detection, strict UTF-8, GBK fallback, wires it into the session read tool, and adds decode/read tests.

PR surface: Source +133, Tests +72. Total +205 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main decodes session read-tool text buffers as UTF-8, and the reported GBK bytes reproduce mojibake under that decode. I did not run a live Chinese Windows OpenClaw session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer/contributor handling for canonical branch selection and real behavior proof; automation cannot provide the contributor's real setup proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(tools): auto-detect file encoding in read tool for GBK support
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92664.

The read tool always decoded files as UTF-8, producing garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files common on Chinese Windows.

## What changed

- Added `decodeFileBuffer()` in `src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.ts` with encoding auto-detection: BOM check → strict UTF-8 → GBK fallback → lenient UTF-8.
- Updated `read.ts` to use `decodeFileBuffer(buffer)` instead of `buffer.toString("utf-8")`.
- No schema change required — encoding detection is transparent.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Read tool displays garbled text (mojibake) when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.
- **Real environment tested**: Node v22.19.0, Linux x64, commit ea1e5c8.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/repro-92664-gbk-encoding.mts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Reproduction for issue #92664 ===
GBK test file bytes: ce c4 bc fe 0a 73 65 63 6f 6e 64

decodeFileBuffer (GBK input):
  BOM detected: none
  Strict UTF-8: FAILED (invalid byte sequence)
  GBK fallback: SUCCESS
  Result: "文件\nsecond"

decodeFileBuffer (UTF-8 input "hello world"):
  BOM detected: none
  Strict UTF-8: SUCCESS
  Result: "hello world"

decodeFileBuffer (UTF-8 BOM input):
  BOM detected: utf-8
  Result: "hello" (BOM stripped)

PASS: GBK-encoded Chinese text correctly decoded.
PASS: UTF-8 files continue to work without regression.
PASS: BOM detection strips BOM before decode.

Read tool integration test:
  GBK file read through createReadToolDefinition()
  Input: 10 bytes GBK → Output: "文件\nsecond"

Test suite: 11 passed, 0 failed
exit code: 0
```
- **Observed result after fix**: GBK-encoded Chinese text (字节 `ce c4 bc fe` → `文件`) is correctly decoded via the GBK fallback. UTF-8 files pass strict validation and render without regression. BOM-prefixed files are detected and BOM is stripped. The read tool integration test confirms end-to-end behavior through `createReadToolDefinition()`.
- **What was not tested**: Big5/Shift_JIS legacy encodings beyond the GBK fallback. Actual Windows console codepage (CP936) end-to-end on a real Chinese Windows machine.
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
